THIS TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 1000 YARDS, A PATH LENGTH OF 17.2 MILES, AND WAS RATED EF2 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 115 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 4:00 PM APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES WEST OF PALO DURO CANYON. THE TORNADO DAMAGED NUMEROUS ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION LINES, SNAPPED POWER POLES, DAMAGED SEVERAL HOMES, OUT BUILDINGS, TREES, AND FENCING. NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

THIS TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 500 YARDS, A PATH LENGTH OF 2.5 MILES, AND WAS RATED EF1 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 90 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 3:36 PM APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES NORTH OF HAPPY. DAMAGE TO A BARN AND SEVERAL POWER LINES WAS OBSERVED. NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

THIS TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 800 YARDS, A PATH LENGTH OF 8.5 MILES, AND WAS RATED EF1 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 110 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 4:14 PM CST APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES NORTHEAST OF PALO DURO CANYON. SEVERAL TRAVEL TRAILERS WERE FLIPPED AND ONE WAS DESTROYED. NONE OF THE TRAILERS WERE ANCHORED. MINOR DAMAGE OCCURRED TO WEAKER STRUCTURES AT THE STATE PARK ENTRANCE, AND THERE WAS NOTABLE TREE DAMAGE. NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

THIS TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 1000 YARDS , A PATH LENGTH OF 12.9 MILES, AND WAS RATED EF0 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 80 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 4:34 PM CST APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES EAST SOUTHEAST OF WASHBURN. THERE WERE FEW DAMAGE INDICATORS AND THE TORNADO WAS LIKELY STRONGER THAN SUGGESTED BY THE VERY LIMITED RATING. THE TORNADO SNAPPED A FEW SMALL DIAMETER POWER POLES, DESTROYED A COUPLE OUT BUILDINGS, AND PUSHED OVER A SEMI TRUCK. NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 6:12 PM CST APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF HOWARDWICK. MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES AND A LOCAL COLLEGE OCCURRED. CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO MOBILE HOMES, RECREATIONAL VEHICLES, AND BOATING FACILITIES. SEVERAL POWER POLES OF VARYING SIZES WERE DOWNED AND MANY TREES SNAPPED IN THE AREA.

MESONET STATION BOISE CITY (BOIS) MEASURED SEVERE WIND GUSTS IN EXCESS OF 65 MPH FOR 25 MINUTES (1030 CDT TO 1055 CDT). THE PEAK WIND GUST MEASURED WAS 78 MPH. LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE REPORTED DOWN THROUGHOUT TOWN AND SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED COMPLETELY. SEVERAL HOMES REPORTED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE AND A PORTION OF THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF THE HIGH SCHOOL AUDITORIUM. DOWNED POWERLINES AND WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGES WERE REPORTED THROUGHOUT TOWN.
